# #725 — Serve wasm binaries via local HTTP for source map stack traces

## Status: open

## Problem

Runtime errors (null pointer deref, illegal cast, unreachable, caught exceptions) show no line numbers because V8 only resolves wasm source maps when loading from a URL, not from an in-memory buffer. This affects ~13,000 failing tests.

Current: `dereferencing a null pointer` (no line info)
Wanted: `dereferencing a null pointer at test.ts:15:3`

## Approach

1. Start a lightweight HTTP server (e.g., `http.createServer`) at test suite startup, serving a temp directory
2. For each test compilation, write `{hash}.wasm` and `{hash}.wasm.map` to the served directory
3. Set `sourceMappingURL={hash}.wasm.map` in the wasm binary's custom section (compiler already emits this — just need the right filename)
4. Instantiate via `WebAssembly.instantiateStreaming(fetch('http://localhost:PORT/{hash}.wasm'), imports)`
5. V8 resolves the source map URL relative to the wasm URL, fetches the `.map`, and includes mapped file/line in stack traces
6. Error handlers read `err.stack` which now contains `test.ts:LINE:COL` instead of bare `wasm-function[N]:0xOFFSET`

## Benefits
- All runtime errors get source line numbers automatically
- No manual source map parsing needed (V8 does it)
- No wasm binary bloat (map is separate file)
- Works with existing `resolveWasmErrorLine` as fallback

## Integration with playground (#644)

The same HTTP server serves:
- `/wasm/{hash}.wasm` + `.map` — for V8 source map resolution
- `/report.html` — conformance dashboard
- `/test262/{path}` — test source files (clickable from report)
- `/playground/` — IDE with conformance panel

Browser can also open wasm source maps in DevTools — click a failing test in the report, see the source with breakpoints.

## Complexity: M
